entries.text: Auto-update window — updates only install during configured hours (default 2:00 AM – 4:00 AM)
Device states persisted to NVS before update reboot — devices restore as HOME/AWAY instead of UNKNOWN after reboot
Pre-update TCP notification sent to Control4 driver before rebooting for update
Control4 driver freezes state changes for 3 minutes after receiving update notification — prevents false automations during reboot
Update window configurable in Settings: start hour, end hour (24h format)
HTTP port now configurable in Settings (default 8090) — access GUI at http://[IP]:8090
Web server default port changed from 80 to 8090
HTTP port change requires reboot to take effect
Fast polling now only activates when ALL tracked devices are absent — previously any one absent device caused 5s polling for everyone, making departures fire in ~50 seconds instead of 10 minutes
Detection timing: 60s polls while anyone is home, 5s fast polls only when house is completely empty
Scanner trigger infinite loop — added 30-second cooldown per device to prevent repeated forced polls when ARP fails
Departure time corrected to ~10 minutes — SUSPECT state now polls at 60s same as PRESENT (was 30s which gave 5.5 min departure)
Detection timing: PRESENT/SUSPECT = 60s polls, ABSENT = 5s fast polls
Default password hint now shows on the login page itself, not after login
Hint is hidden after first successful login so it doesn't clutter the screen for returning users
Removed duplicate loginError element that was causing silent login failures
Login syntax error — orphaned OUI array content was breaking JavaScript on fresh installs
Wrong password now shows error message with default password hint
First login shows toast: 'Default password is changeme — change it in Settings'
Critical: scanner was triggering a full 254-IP scan every 2 seconds — caused by getDevices() auto-triggering scans on every call
Random missed polls eliminated — scanner mass ARP probing was interfering with detection engine ARP probes
Scanner now uses read-only cache for detection cross-reference — full scans only on scheduled intervals
Departure time increased to ~10 minutes (10 misses × 60s) — avoids false aways for quick trips
ABSENT devices now fast poll at 5s for return detection — arrival detection much faster
SUSPECT devices poll at 30s (intermediate between normal 60s and fast 5s)
Scanner-triggered detection — when scanner finds an ABSENT device's MAC, immediately triggers a poll
Removed debug prints from _updateAbsentMacs
Critical: fast poll was running at 30 seconds not 5 — FAST_POLL_INTERVAL_MS corrected to 5000ms
Normal poll interval corrected to 60 seconds (was 3 minutes)
Detection now handles DHCP IP changes via mDNS and scanner cache
ARP probe checks scanner cache for updated IP when stored IP fails
Scanner results now sorted — tracked devices first, then likely phones, then unknown
Background scan interval reduced from 5 minutes to 2 minutes for faster network discovery
Scanner ARP passes increased from 3 to 5 per cycle for better device coverage
mDNS timeout reduced from 800ms to 200ms — detection cycles no longer blocked
mDNS detection timeout reduced from 800ms to 200ms — poll cycles no longer blocked for 3+ seconds
mDNS hostname now correctly matches iOS format (apostrophes removed, not kept as '-s-')
Scanner phone detection now includes Samsung, Google Pixel, and OnePlus OUI prefixes
Scanner correctly identifies locally administered MACs (randomized) as likely phones
Firmware version now included in every TCP state push — C4 driver populates it on first message
Passive wake debug logging added to identify watchlist population failure
Return confidence threshold lowered to 70
VERSION TCP command — returns firmware version without HTTP auth
C4 driver now gets firmware version over TCP instead of HTTP — fixes version not showing in Composer
Passive wake was never triggering — _updateAbsentMacs was called on arrival but not departure
Passive sniffer watchlist now correctly populated when device goes ABSENT
Health endpoint now returns firmware_version field — fixes Control4 driver not showing firmware version
Fast poll interval reduced from 15s to 5s — return detection now sub-10 seconds
Added debug logging to passive wake mechanism to diagnose trigger issues
Check for Updates button in Firmware tab — instant version check without waiting 24 hours
Passive wake detection — when ABSENT device sends any WiFi packet, detection runs immediately instead of waiting for next poll cycle
Return detection now near-instant — phone sends mDNS/broadcast on joining WiFi which wakes detection within seconds
Miss threshold default lowered to 8 minutes
Miss threshold reduced from 15 to 8 — departure now takes ~8 minutes instead of 15
Fast poll kept active after ABSENT for quicker return detection
Fast poll interval reduced from 30s to 15s
Device ID field removed from Add Device modal — auto-generated from friendly name (e.g. 'Jacob\'s iPhone' becomes 'jacobs_iphone')
Simpler device setup — dealers only need to enter name, MAC, and IP
Watchdog no longer triggers during OTA firmware download — suspended during download and re-enabled on failure
Device stays online after failed update attempt
Device no longer reboots when update download fails — stays online and keeps banner visible for retry
Device only reboots after a successful firmware flash
Automatic OTA updates from GitHub — checks every 24 hours for new firmware
Auto-update toggle in Settings — off by default, enable for hands-off dealer installs
Update available banner on Dashboard when new version is detected
Install Update button for manual one-click updates without USB cable
FIRMWARE_VERSION constant — single source of truth for version number
Devices tab now shows live miss count in Nerd Facts
Miss counter no longer increments past threshold when device is already ABSENT
Duplicate event suppression now stops callbacks too — Control4 and HA no longer receive repeated departure events
Suppressed events no longer forwarded to TCP, MQTT, or any other integration
Departure events no longer fire repeatedly every poll cycle once device is ABSENT — was causing repeated TCP/MQTT/C4 events every minute
Miss counter now increments silently after ABSENT threshold is reached — no callbacks fired
Root cause of repeated every-6-minute events in event log resolved
False arrival events from passive WiFi traffic eliminated — requires confidence 80+ and 2 consecutive hits to return from ABSENT
Network scanner phone detection moved to browser-side with expanded Apple OUI list — now correctly identifies iPhones and iPads
Duplicate event suppression checks 5 minute window across all recent events
Duplicate event suppression now checks a 5 minute window instead of just the last event
Multiple devices departing simultaneously no longer causes repeated events every 3 minutes
Poll Now button no longer causes reboot — was running detection from wrong task context
Force poll now queued as a flag and executed safely by the detection task on Core 0
Event timeline no longer shows repeated departure/arrival events every 3 minutes
Duplicate consecutive events for the same device are suppressed from the history buffer
Device add date stored in NVS — persists across reboots
Nerd Facts now shows date device was added
Wizard step titles renamed to natural names — Welcome, Security, MAC Randomization, Add Devices, Detection Settings, All Done
Restored 'no subscription' text in wizard welcome screen
Apple device detection now recognizes iPads, Macs, Apple Watch, and AirPods with real MACs — not just iPhones with randomized MACs
Wizard resume button now actually appears — HTML element was missing from the DOM
Resume Setup Wizard button shows after adding a device from within the wizard flow
Network scanner no longer limited to 10 devices — was hitting ESP32 ARP table size limit
Scanner now maintains its own persistent device cache — results accumulate across scans
Three-pass ARP table read catches more devices before entries get evicted
Cached entries expire after 10 minutes to keep results fresh
Network scanner was limited to 10 devices due to JSON document size — now supports up to 50
Network scanner results were inconsistent — fixed broken ARP table loop that was only checking one entry
Tracked device highlighting now works reliably — fixed MAC address case comparison
Background ARP scanner task — probes all IPs in subnet every 5 minutes
Network scanner now finds all devices on the network, not just the router
Scanner shows last scan time and device count
Scanner auto-refreshes if first scan is still in progress
RSSI chart font size and label padding improved
RSSI chart now renders correctly when Health tab is opened
Canvas no longer resized inside draw function which was clearing it before drawing
Auto theme icon changed to monitor symbol — much clearer than sync arrows
RSSI chart empty message now shows how many readings have been collected so far
RSSI chart rendering delayed 50ms to ensure canvas dimensions are available
Network Scanner tab — shows all devices on local network, highlights tracked devices and likely phones, one-click to add
WiFi signal history chart in Health tab — 1 hour RSSI trend with quality zones
Event timeline grouped by date with actual timestamps instead of relative time
Onboarding wizard — 6-step setup guide, auto-shows on first login when no devices configured
Setup Wizard button in Settings for re-running wizard anytime
Theme toggle now uses SVG icon that adapts to light/dark mode with no blue box
Default miss threshold: 5 → 15
Miss threshold is now global — applies to all devices from Settings
Network scanner moved into Devices tab instead of standalone tab — better workflow for adding devices
Network scanner now uses simpler ARP table lookup — no more infinite loading spinner
Network scanner now actively sends ARP requests before reading table — finds more devices
RSSI chart now displays correctly even when NTP timestamp is 0
Wizard pauses when opening scanner or add device — Resume Setup button appears to continue
Theme icons improved — clearer sun, moon, and sync symbols with better visibility
Wizard welcome text updated — removed misleading 'works offline' claim
Nerd Facts panels stay open through dashboard refreshes and Poll Now
MQTT status chip in header — shows green/red connection state when MQTT is enabled
Header indicator selector in Settings — toggle C4, MQTT, and uptime badge on or off
Header chip preferences saved to browser localStorage
Nerd Facts miss count now shows 'threshold reached' when device is absent instead of misleading 0
Miss count now displays as current/threshold (e.g. 3/5) while device is in suspect state
Settings fields that require reboot now labeled with amber reboot indicator
MQTT memory leak — WiFiClient and PubSubClient now recreated fresh on each reconnect attempt
Leaked buffers from failed MQTT connections now properly freed via delete before each reconnect
All MQTT client dereferences now null-checked to prevent crashes during client recreation
Nerd Facts now works correctly on both Dashboard and Devices tabs
Fixed ID conflict causing Nerd Facts to not expand on Devices tab
Arrival and departure toast notifications — green when someone arrives, red when everyone leaves, info when a person departs
Global occupancy change toasts — House is now occupied / Everyone away
Crash loop on boot — increased FreeRTOS task stack sizes to prevent stack overflow
Detection task stack: 8KB to 12KB
MQTT task stack: 8KB to 10KB
TCP and watchdog tasks: 4KB to 6KB each
Dark mode, light mode, and auto (follows system) — toggle in header
Persistent login — browser remembers session token, no re-login on refresh
Splash screen login — centered modal with fade transition
Nerd Facts — expandable per-device panel showing detection method, confidence, miss count, last seen
C4 TCP connection status dot in header — green when Control4 is connected
Uptime badge in header — shows days/hours of continuous uptime
Toast notifications — replaces all alert() dialogs with subtle slide-in toasts
Tab order: Dashboard, Devices, Events, Settings, Health, Firmware Update, Changelog
TCP client count shown in Health tab
Memory leak in passive traffic detection cache — entries now cleaned up after 5 minutes and cache capped at 64 entries
Heap delta shown in watchdog heartbeat — easy to spot memory trends
Warning logged when free heap drops below 50KB
Warning logged when heap drops more than 5KB in one minute
Device IP address shown in GUI header — always visible without needing the health tab
Portal success page now shows both hostname.local and IP address
Reset WiFi button in Settings — launches setup portal on next boot without touching code
Portal redirects to IP address after setup instead of hostname.local for reliability on Windows
Captive portal scan now retries up to 8 times with increasing delays — much more reliable on Windows laptops
Scan results cached for 30 seconds — rapid retries get instant results without re-scanning
Save request auto-retries up to 2 times on network error before showing failure
Scan failure shows inline Try Again link instead of requiring full page refresh
Watchdog reboot caused by MQTT connect() blocking — added 3s socket timeout
MQTT reconnect interval increased from 5s to 15s to reduce blocking frequency
Watchdog-safe yield added before and after MQTT connect attempts
NTP timezone race condition — local time now displays correctly on first boot instead of showing UTC
Firmware version inconsistency — all version references now correctly show 1.6.1
Version number in GUI header now matches actual firmware version
OTA firmware update via web GUI — no USB cable required
OTA filesystem update via web GUI — update GUI and changelog remotely
Progress bar during upload with automatic reconnect after reboot
Automatic rollback if firmware update fails or verification fails
Firmware Update tab in web GUI with step-by-step instructions
MQTT username and password fields in settings GUI
MQTT enabled flag not persisting correctly across reboots
GUI header version now dynamic from firmware
Reboot button in settings — no more unplugging to apply changes
MQTT enabled flag not persisting correctly across reboots
GUI header version now pulls dynamically from firmware instead of hardcoded
Health tab firmware version corrected to current version
Reboot button in Settings — applies config changes without unplugging device
POST /api/v1/reboot endpoint
MQTT client with PubSubClient library
Publishes occupancy, per-device state, events, and health to MQTT broker
Home Assistant MQTT auto-discovery — entities appear automatically in HA
Retained topics for occupancy, device state, and health
MQTT last-will message — HA shows device offline if ESP32 drops
MQTT command topics: cmd/poll and cmd/restart
MQTT connection status shown in health tab and watchdog heartbeat
mqttTask on Core 1 — isolated from detection and TCP tasks
NTP time synchronization — events now carry real Unix timestamps
Timezone selection in settings — Eastern, Central, Mountain, Pacific, Hawaii, Alaska, Arizona
NTP sync status and current time shown in health tab
Current time shown in watchdog heartbeat log every 60s
Timezone saved to NVS and restored on reboot
WiFi captive portal for first-time setup — no code changes needed for new installs
Setup AP broadcasts as PresenceSetup_XXXX unique to each device
Portal auto-scans and lists available networks with signal strength
Captive portal detection for iOS, Android, and Windows — opens automatically
Automatic fallback to portal if WiFi fails after 3 connection attempts
Portal redirects to device URL after successful credential save
Hardcoded WiFi credentials removed from firmware
TCP socket server on port 4999 for Control4 and other platform drivers
Push notifications to all TCP clients on every presence event
Full state push to new clients immediately on connect
POLL command — client requests immediate full status
PING/PONG keepalive support
Up to 4 simultaneous TCP client connections
REST API with full CRUD for devices, status, events, health, config, backup/restore
Web GUI served from LittleFS — dashboard, devices, events, settings, health, changelog tabs
Session token authentication for all API endpoints
Boot delay before first poll increased from 3s to 8s
Multi-method detection engine: ARP, mDNS/Bonjour, passive traffic, ICMP ping
Per-device state machine: UNKNOWN to PRESENT to SUSPECT to ABSENT
Global occupancy state, event system, relay output, LEDs, NVS config, watchdog
